item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Was inspired by PeppersGhost 's recent tale to come take a second look at this. (BTW the tale is quite well done, pls go look at it.)

I just don't get the hype with this, and the feeling has only gotten stronger as time wears on. It's a creepy inversion of Santa. That's all there is, beyond some very creative squick. And the quick is very good, but there doesn't seem to be anything driving it. That tone even extends to the character itself, creating the impression that Santa really is just bustin' heads for shock value. Even the addendum, which was an excellent chance to round out the narrative and give us a finishing reveal, just adds more gore and Santa comparisons. It's like drinking from a fire hose, but the fire hose is spouting children's body parts and pine resin. It's frustratingly shallow once you push past it all.

That being said, I guess it's really effective at what it's trying to do and be. Can't argue with that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This strangely looked like a version of SCP-106 but with:
1: More detail.
2: The foundation can't even put it in a box where the foundation at least know the civilian can't found about.
3: More incunfartable/intimadiing.

item.title: Icelandic Influence?
item.content:encoded: I've recently gained knowledge of the Yule Cat of Icelandic Christmas folklore. Was this SCP potentially inspired by it, by any chance?
This article is one of the few to make me so incredibly disgusted and uncomfortable. Wonderful job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This SCP just gave me chills. Well done.

+1

item.title: Re: CHRISTMAS CAME LATE THIS YEAR (4K Entry)
item.content:encoded:
Fuck you I was planning on sleeping tonight.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This and SCP-2852 are the only SCPs that have genuinely creeped me out. The fact that you can scare me with something that I know doesn’t exist is astonishing. I hate it because it creeps me out so much but I love it for the exact same reason, great job

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent amount of terror going on. One thing was a little unclear to me, may just be me: does it spent all twelve days going after one family, or can it do multiple families within the same twelve days? I'm just a little confused on that one point, though the former makes more sense to me, the latter sounds more like the story Ekaterina told.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Utterly gripping and truly sickening and horrific. I almost can't bring myself to upvote it, given how thoroughly unpleasant I found reading it. But it's hit me harder than any SCP I've read in a long time, so it deserves a vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When you can’t make the toys, you become the toys.

thats so sinister. i love it. i love the whole scp; but the interview it what sold me. shes used to being abused, and it was portrayed so well. its heartbreaking to see someone so desensitized to how poorly their treated - ive met a few people like that - and i imagine its hard to write, but you did an amazing job. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Awesome SCP! just watched the movie "sinister" (great film btw to anyone who hasn't seen it) even though this was more akin to Krampus than Bughuul, I just couldn't shake those vibes.

item.title: Re: CHRISTMAS CAME LATE THIS YEAR (4K Entry)
item.content:encoded: I've got to say you did an excellent job with this SCP. There are a lot of frightening SCPs out there but none of them had me genuinely creeped out while reading them like this one did. Writing an SCP that's powerful and can destroy life as we know it is easy. Writing an SCP that can creep out the reader while it poses no threat to them is impressive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Loved it. The last entry made me think a bit of the Russian Doll Maker, which is certainly a good thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i blame 239. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just came here to say that I wish all SCP were actually written in a style like this.

It reads like something actual scientists might write. It's very precise and empirical. It addresses a lot of the questions that pop up as you read it, where other SCPs tend to leave you with a "I wonder what happened if…" / "why didn't they test this and that" sort of feeling.

And the content itself is creepy af as well. Great job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I… was not expecting to read something like this when I first clicked the link. Sure, this article did not make me afraid of the entity, but I did feel a little unnerved about it. This thing makes toys out of Children, and that is probably one of the few things that scare me. Anything that can be related to cannibalism I find scary, and it isn't the story that I find scary either. It's the concept. Really, try it right now. Imagine having your lips sown shut for a week. Could you honestly say that you could last that long without it? Now imagine your skin being ripped from you, as you slowly get skinned alive. Do you think that you can bear that pain? The answer is no, you can't. No-one can, and that is what made this thing so interesting to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh Dear

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm gonna go cry into my sheets now…

Oh shit this guy can still get me there. Better move my entire family south!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love stuff like this and I don’t know if that makes me ok in the head. Take my +1 and never make something like this again. (Unless you want to)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm in awe, i know it doesn't mean much coming from me, but this is amazing! Well I mean the yule man isn't but you know what I mean. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Lost it at the very last line and started to weep. +1

item.title: Scary Christmas
item.content:encoded: Consider me shaken to the core. Rare skip that terrifies and makes you want to read more. Personally would love expansion on the pagan ritual lore.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is why people think we’re all mentally disturbed.

+1

item.title: Re: CHRISTMAS CAME LATE THIS YEAR (4K Entry)
item.content:encoded:
Happy holidays!
I loved this. This is a scary SCP that makes me want to read more, I fear for my life and this is one of my favorites.

item.title: Merry Christmas!
item.content:encoded: Brilliantly written and absolutely terrifying! Exactly what is needed on Christmas Eve. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That was a terrifying and deeply disturbing SCP.

Nicely written! +1

item.title: Re: CHRISTMAS CAME LATE THIS YEAR (4K Entry)
item.content:encoded: The Best Fifth Serie SCP I read so far. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Played it very close to the Santa mythos in a twisted way I see. Def an interesting read, dark twists. A good Christmas article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So uh… I got a PM about this from someone who found the comment disturbing.

OP, I presume this is a parody-ish sort of song meant to be sung to the tune of "Mr. Sandman" by The Chordettes? If not uh… maybe put that in a collapsible I guess. Not sure if it's necessarily NSFW, but it's close. /me shrugs

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can I upvote comments

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dear Lord.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mister Yule Man,
Bring me a bone,
Covered in snow, from the mesocyclone
Bring me a skull, rosy cheeks still connected
Bring me an only child half-dissected
Mister Yule Man,
Stalking the cold
The greatest guest in our festive household
Starting with Father, strung up by his tendons
His screams of agony are most horrendous
Mr. Yule Man,
Cutting with glee
Take out my organs, for any to see,
Give me a hole where my terrified face was
Make me lie down and beg for eu-tha-nasia

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
fuck
(+1)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well this is fucking horrifying

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I switched browsers to sign in and upvote this article.

I distinctly remember downvoting a different article for being unnecessary torture porn. This article is nearly as gory, but the theme/context of the story makes me consider this “necessary torture porn.” Weird now that works, huh?

Good job. I’ll be reading this for the channel because it’s got the sick creeps and I’m hella impressed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy fucking shit. Did this shit get sent forward in time from the 3000 contest or something!?

fuck

item.title: Re: CHRISTMAS CAME LATE THIS YEAR (4K Entry)
item.content:encoded:
VERY good entry!!!! I'm not getting sleep tonight.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A complex web of murder, gore, and abductions that lead to different clues all around the world that point to a fictional entity depicted as a classic Christmas folklore which can take different shapes all to terrify its own victims. It's just a good SCP, it personally really inspires me to read even more, how it is written or how solid the horror is. +1, amazing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

SCP-4666

… I can't believe it took me this long to notice that.

item.title: Re: From SCP-239
item.content:encoded: Honestly I find the idea of Santa Claus being based on SCP-4666 more disturbing and interesting than a reality bender doing it the other way around.

item.title: Re: From SCP-239
item.content:encoded:
This would be an excellent concept for a tale, if not for the fact that the vast majority of the murders occurred before 2004.
Unless… 239 can retroactively change past events. But that would be cra-
oh. oh no.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My guess is that listening to the Yule Man speak it so much she was able to pick up some phrases in the language

item.title: From SCP-239
item.content:encoded: Note from Dr. ████████, dated 12/26/04: Who the hell thought it would be a good idea to tell her about "Santa Claus" and then tell her that it was just a story?! Now we have another potential SCP to deal with, but we can't catch him because he is "magic".
CHRISTMAS CAME EARLY THIS YEAR

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No punches pulled. Pure, unadulterated horror.

Easy plus one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Considering that it can manifest seemingly anywhere on the planet I doubt it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really liked this. Though I do wonder what happens if a family left the area after the stalking began. Would they be safe?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really fucking haunting. This is up there with my favorite SCPs ever. Just an excellent read, man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Same. In fact, it reminds me of a GOC member group origin story idea I had:

  • A child is kidnapped by the Fae (or some similar creatures) and taken to their city via a magic gateway.
  • She escapes a few years later and forms a group dedicated to protecting children from the Fae.
  • At first, they just block as many Fae gateways as they can and attack Fae that try to steal children.
  • But later, they decide this isn't enough: to make sure no child is threatened by the Fae ever again, they cross into the Fae city and … give them the GOC treatment.
  • Later, she is assuring her daughter the Fae aren't a threat to her…not anymore.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Intense stuff. Never have wanted the GOC to curb stomp something so much.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is possibly the most horrifying entry I've read. It's like the polar opposite of 231, which relies on obfuscation to horrify. This has no redactions at all, but you sometimes wish it did. Extremely well written and truly evil.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This one of my favourite SCP's. Super good.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now I wish I had read this SCP back in December. Pretty damn disturbing. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is so good, but quick point in the item log where it says "Drum skin consists of a 390 cm³ piece of skin belonging to a human child…" It should be 390cm2 if I'm not mistaken. 390 cm3 would be quite a large chunk of flesh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was very well written, horrific but well written +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pre-Proto-Germanic is Germanic languages as they would have been before the first germanic sound shift.
Since the Santa Claus myth is based on early Germanic mythology and the head god of the Germanics (Wodan) it would make sense for the real Santa Claus to speak such an ancient language.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this, but I'm really intrigued by the proto language the girl speaks at the end. I feel like I'm missing something, or there's a mystery left unsolved…?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this scp scares the everliving shit out of me- i love it

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reverse santa is best scp. Holy fucking shit, what did i just read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, is it bad that I didn't retch or even feel queasy not at one point in this article? I think it's because i'm a dweller of r/enoughinternet, but this is still terrifying. +1.

I also really want to see your 3000 contest draft.

edit: r/enoughinternet has been deleted, wahoo
